description: Extend the integration test framework with a pool of pre-warmed TestContext instances, EVM snapshot/revert for clean chain state between tests, a golden-file comparison pipeline with pluggable normalizers, and a declarative IntegrationTest struct for multi-step CLI test scenarios.

userStories.title: Normalizer Trait and Built-in Normalizers
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a Normalizer trait and built-in normalizers that strip non-deterministic content (ANSI codes, addresses, hashes, timestamps, versions, etc.) from CLI output so that golden file comparisons are stable across environments and runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Normalizer trait is defined with normalize(&self, input: &str) -> String

All 9 normalizers are implemented: ColorNormalizer, AddressNormalizer, HashNormalizer, TimestampNormalizer, VersionNormalizer, GitCommitNormalizer, RepositoryIdNormalizer, SpinnerNormalizer, ForgeWarningNormalizer
NormalizerChain::default() returns a chain with all built-in normalizers, color first, then hash before address
Unit test: ColorNormalizer strips ANSI escape codes
Unit test: AddressNormalizer replaces 40-hex-char addresses, HashNormalizer replaces 64-hex-char hashes without matching addresses
Unit test: TimestampNormalizer handles ISO-8601 and relative time strings
Unit test: NormalizerChain applies normalizers in sequence
Module registered in framework/mod.rs, regex added to dev-dependencies
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: Golden File Comparison
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a GoldenFile struct that compares normalized CLI output against baseline files in tests/golden/ and supports auto-updating baselines via UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 so that I can validate CLI output without manual file management.
acceptanceCriteria: GoldenFile::compare() reads from tests/golden/{test_name}/{case_name}.golden
Matching content passes silently; mismatched content panics with a unified diff
Missing golden file without UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 panics with instructive message
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 writes actual content to golden file and creates parent directories
compare_normalized() applies normalizer chain before comparison
Unit test: round-trip — write golden file via UPDATE_GOLDEN, then compare succeeds
Unit test: mismatch produces a readable diff message
similar crate added to dev-dependencies, module registered in framework/mod.rs
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: EVM Snapshot Utilities for Pool Release
userStories.description: As a test framework developer, I want snapshot/revert helper functions wrapping AnvilNode's snapshot/revert APIs so that the pool can restore clean chain state on context release.
acceptanceCriteria: take_snapshot(node) returns a U256 snapshot ID from an AnvilNode
revert_snapshot(node, id) reverts to a given snapshot ID and errors if revert returns false
take_snapshots(nodes) snapshots all nodes in a HashMap<String, AnvilNode>
revert_snapshots(nodes, ids) reverts all nodes to their saved IDs
Async test: snapshot → modify state → revert → state is restored
Module registered in framework/mod.rs
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: Workspace Cleanup Utility
userStories.description: As a test framework developer, I want a clean_workspace() function that resets a TestWorkdir to a pristine state by removing build artifacts and treb state so that the next test in the pool gets a clean workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: clean_workspace() removes .treb/, broadcast/, cache/, out/ directories
clean_workspace() cleans script/deploy/ but preserves .gitkeep
.treb/ is recreated as an empty directory after removal
Missing directories are silently skipped (best-effort cleanup)
Test: create dummy files in .treb/ and out/, call clean_workspace(), verify they're gone
Test: verify foundry.toml and src/ are NOT removed
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: ContextPool with Acquire/Release
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a ContextPool that pre-creates N TestContext instances with Anvil nodes and provides thread-safe acquire/release with automatic EVM snapshot revert and workspace cleanup so that parallel tests share amortized Anvil startup cost with clean state.
acceptanceCriteria: ContextPool::new(2, "minimal-project") creates 2 contexts with 2 Anvil nodes each (chain IDs 31337 and 31338)
ContextPool::acquire() returns a PoolGuard that derefs to TestContext
When all contexts are in use, acquire() blocks until one is released
Dropping PoolGuard reverts EVM snapshots, cleans workspace, takes fresh snapshots, and returns context to pool
default_size() reads RAYON_NUM_THREADS or falls back to CPU count via num_cpus
Async test: acquire context, drop guard, acquire again — second acquire has clean chain state
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: IntegrationTest Struct and Runner
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a declarative IntegrationTest struct with builder methods and run_integration_test()/run_integration_tests() entry points so that I can define multi-step CLI test scenarios that execute commands in order and compare output against golden files.
acceptanceCriteria: IntegrationTest struct has all required fields: name, pre_setup, setup_cmds, post_setup, test_cmds, post_test, expect_err, extra_normalizers, output_artifacts, skip_golden
Builder methods allow ergonomic construction (e.g., IntegrationTest::new("test").setup(&["init"]).test(&["list"]))
run_integration_test() executes commands in order: pre_setup → setup → post_setup → test → post_test
expect_err: true asserts non-zero exit status for test commands
Output artifacts are read from workdir and included in golden output when files exist
skip_golden: true skips golden file comparison; extra_normalizers are appended to default chain
Unit test: construct IntegrationTest with builder, verify field values
run_integration_tests() runs all tests in sequence
cargo check --workspace passes

userStories.title: Pool + Golden File Smoke Test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an end-to-end smoke test that exercises the full Phase 3 pipeline — pool acquire/release, golden file comparison, and IntegrationTest struct — so that the framework is validated as a whole before downstream phases depend on it.
acceptanceCriteria: pool_acquire_release_clean_state test passes: pool creates context, acquire/release cycle works, state is clean after release
golden_file_round_trip test passes: treb version output matches golden file after normalization
integration_test_struct_smoke test passes: IntegrationTest + run_integration_test() works end-to-end with skip_golden: true
All tests pass with cargo test --workspace
Golden file(s) are committed to tests/golden/
